So the other great way to use phone sales is to sell an appointment deposit. So an appointment deposit would be for a high ticket close, so if you're trying to sell someone in person for like let's say a $2,000 or $3,000 12 week transformation, the way to do it is not to try and sell them that over the phone or even sell them that in person on the first try, but actually you sell a $99 appointment deposit so that they value the appointment and it positions the closer, if that's you, or someone else to actually close the sale, all right? So you're gonna take them all the way to the end and you say, listen, I can't actually give you the specifics, but I can reserve a time because it seems like you qualify and then boom, you set the appointment for $99 that's not refundable. You're positioned to close and that's how you can close $2,000 or $3,000 tickets. The second point there is just that if you have somebody who's not as good as good at phone sales, you can still get that higher price point that's like, you know, like I'd say a $500 price point and just have them set, basically have people commit to it and just put $99 down to hold their spot and then when they come in, you can kind of finish off the sale because at that point, they already know the price, they've already put it down. It's easier for people who are not as good at phone sales to just close the deposit to say, is that if you were selling continuity, so let's say someone's inside of a front end play for you, let's say you did an LBO for whatever, it's like a little barrier offer, like a $21, 21 day type thing. So here's some of the advantages to doing this. One, you can sell all day long, which is awesome rather than just selling in the afternoon hours, which is usually when most people have to do it because of traffic, et cetera. Second thing is that it's closest to interest, so if you're coming in for cold leads or you have cold leads that are coming in from traffic, the moment someone's ops in is the moment that their interest is highest and then it slowly degrades over time, it actually quickly degrades over time, that first 10 minutes is when it's the highest and then it quickly goes out and then it withers out. So the more likely you are going to close them. The nice thing here is also when you do phone sales, you don't have hordes of people sitting in your waiting room, sitting in your office, or really, we don't have a market side, but like the gym on the side of getting more customers, this can be a big problem as having too many people like sitting around because you're trying to finish off a sale. So when you're doing phone sales, this is a great way to not have to deal with that as you're selling from home. The other nice thing is that you can take breaks when you need to, you can take a water break, you can move around and if you don't have any appointments, you can dial out, outbound to try and get more sales when it's convenient for you rather than just having to sit there and be bombarded by people walking in and taking sales meetings.
